title: Professional/Chartered company secretaries
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate accounting, investing, banking, insurance, securities, and other financial activities of a branch, office, or department of an establishment. Direct financial activities, such as planning, procurement, and investments for all or part of an organization. Plan, direct, or coordinate investment strategy or operations for a large pool of liquid assets supplied by institutional investors or individual investors. (Based on 9 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Plan, direct, or coordinate the activities of workers in branches, offices, or departments of establishments.
Manage investment funds to maximize return on client investments by selecting specific investments or investment mixes.
Prepare or direct preparation of financial statements, business activity reports, budgets, or regulatory reports.
Monitor financial activities, cash flow, and reserve levels to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements.
Establish and maintain relationships with customers, providing assistance with accounting and budgeting policies.
Evaluate the potential of new product developments or market opportunities based on business plans and market potential.
Develop or implement investment policies or strategies, monitoring financial performance to ensure portfolios meet goals.
Conduct audits of accounts and financial transactions to ensure compliance with regulations and organizational policies.
Recruit and evaluate staff members, overseeing training programs in budgeting and financial management areas.
Approve, reject, or coordinate loan applications, ensuring conformance with strategic goals or regulatory requirements.
Receive and process payments from customers, using electronic transaction services.
Submit project deliverables to clients and communicate with key stakeholders to determine project requirements and objectives.
Schedule or facilitate project meetings and assign duties to project personnel.
Confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems, monitoring their performance to provide feedback.
Create project status presentations for delivery to customers and prepare progress or cost tracking reports.
Develop or update project plans including objectives, technologies, schedules, funding, and staffing.
Identify, review, or select vendors or consultants to meet project needs and negotiate with stakeholders to obtain resources.
Monitor project milestones, deliverables, and costs incurred by project staff to identify budget issues and ensure deadlines are met.
Plan, schedule, or coordinate project activities and recruit or hire project personnel as needed.
Propose, review, or approve modifications to project plans and report project status to managers.
Identify project needs such as resources, staff, or finances by reviewing objectives and schedules.
